title: ambrosia linearis
intro: Ambrosia linearis, also known as Mexican ragweed or narrow-leaved bur-sage, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family that is native to Mexico and the southwestern United States. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are said to help soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Ambrosia linearis include its potential to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as asthma and bronchitis, as well as its ability to reduce fever and alleviate pain. Ambrosia linearis is typically prepared as a tea or infused oil, and is sometimes used in herbal remedies to treat various health conditions.
